review: 1.10.0-RC4c mesh hot-path robustness — re-review of spec v2 (design-review patches)
method: 3 fold-checks (M1-M3) + 2 new-flaw probes + skeptic verify; 8 agents

verdict.round1MustFixes: M1 (backoff DEGRADED latch), M2 (unicast silent-drop), M3 (zero-Redis over-claim) — ALL resolvedInV2 + verifiedAgainstActualCode (designs sound + implementable; impl not yet written)
verdict.newFinding: 1 MAJOR in the v2 unicast fallback + 2 minor — folded as v2.1

newMajorFolded.severity: MAJOR (blocksPlanning; cost/storm, not a correctness regression)
newMajorFolded.title: v2 unicast snapshot-miss fallback never warmed peerSnapshot → per-message SCAN storm on fresh-target join
newMajorFolded.detail: The v2 fallback assigned the resolved address to a LOCAL var and never wrote this.peerSnapshot (refreshed only by the ~tickMs membership tick). RedisMeshNodeDirectory.peers() is always a full SCAN MATCH netty:mesh:addr:* + per-key GET (no single-key lookup). So every unicast to a target absent from the last snapshot (a freshly-joined node) re-paid a full SCAN, per-message, for ≤tickMs — N sessions on a fresh node = N per-message control-plane SCANs on every node join / autoscale event, re-introducing exactly the per-message directory SCAN BL5 exists to remove. The §8 risk-#1 'single bounded read, rare' framing masked it. Distinct from round-1 finding #2 (which fixed the silent-DROP correctness gap; this is the fallback's cost profile).
newMajorFolded.fix: The fallback warms the whole snapshot from its directory.peers() read (this.peerSnapshot = fresh) — ≤1 SCAN per fresh target per window, then snapshot hits. §8 risk #1 corrected. Folded v2.1 §3.1/§8.

minorsFolded: M1 retainAll prune must materialize live = peers.keySet() − deadNodeView.get() (not plain peers.keySet()) so a dead-but-advertised peer's backoff entry is dropped immediately, matching §3.2's live∩address wording (v2.1 §3.2)
§1 'zero Redis on the hot path' headline scoped explicitly to broadcast+interest; unicast's snapshot+bounded-fallback noted inline (v2.1 §1)
alsoNoted: The M1/M2/M3 fixes exist only in the spec; HEAD is still RC4b code (no connectionForSend, no reconnect map, publish/unicast still join() on the hot path). 'Resolved' = the v2 design is sound + implementable; the plan (task #10) treats them as designs-to-implement.
selfReviewPreReview: Before this re-review, a self-review caught that connectionForSend gated a cached-active channel the tick had reconnected — fixed so the backoff gates only a fresh DIAL (v2.1 §3.2).
